name: Salmon Patties with Creamed Peas
recipeIngredient: 1 cup crushed white soda crackers (or 1 cup crushed Saltines)
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the salmon and place it in a mixing bowl.
2. Remove bones and dark skin from the salmon and set aside.
3. Add egg whites, chopped onion, and salt to the salmon juice and mix together with a fork.
4. Crush the crackers with your hands (do not use cracker crumbs) and add them to the egg white mixture along with the remaining salmon.
5. Mix well until combined; the cracker pieces and small chunks of salmon should still be visible.
6. Shape into patties on a greased cookie sheet.
7. Bake at 450°F for 35 to 40 minutes, or until firm and lightly browned.
8. In a small saucepan, stir together chicken broth and cornstarch until smooth.
9. Cook over moderate heat until thickened and smooth, stirring continuously for another minute.
10. Add margarine and peas to the sauce and mix lightly.
11. Reheat to serving temperature.
12. Serve 1 patty with 1/6 of the sauce (about 1/3 cup) per serving.
13. Food Exchange: 1 BREAD EXCHANGE + 2 LEAN MEAT EXCHANGE
14. CAL: 204; CHO: 14 gm; PRO: 10 gm
15. For low-sodium diets, omit salt and use salt-free margarine, low-sodium crackers, and fresh or frozen peas. Discard salmon juice and substitute an equal amount of water.
Source: The New Diabetic Cookbook by Mabel Cavaiani; R.D. Brought to you and yours via Nancy O'Brion and her Meal-Master.
